item.title: Legge expects tough battle against Brumbies
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/06/2026 The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women are expecting a physical contest when they host the Brumbies in Ba tomorrow in Round 3 of the Super Rugby Women's competition.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/06/2026

The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women are expecting a physical contest when they host the Brumbies in Ba tomorrow in Round 3 of the Super Rugby Women's competition.

Coach Mike Legge says the team is anticipating a tough battle up front and will need to be strong in contact, particularly around the rucks and at set-piece time.

He says the Drua must remain consistent at the breakdown and believes that if they can maintain that consistency for the full 80 minutes, they can achieve a positive result.

The Drua Women and the Brumbies are both on four points in the standings, while the Waratahs lead the competition with eight points.

The Drua Women will take on the Brumbies at 2.05pm tomorrow at 4R Stadium in Ba.

item.title: Defending champions Labasa hold Suva to a 1-all draw in Fiji FACT
item.description: It was a thrilling encounter at Subrail Park as defending champions Labasa fought back late to hold Suva to a 1-all draw in their first pool match of the BiC Fiji FACT. Suva got off to a strong start and opened the scoring in the first half through Siotame Kubu to take a deserved 1-0 lead.
item.content:encoded:
It was a thrilling encounter at Subrail Park as defending champions Labasa fought back late to hold Suva to a 1-all draw in their first pool match of the BiC Fiji FACT.

Suva got off to a strong start and opened the scoring in the first half through Siotame Kubu to take a deserved 1-0 lead.

Despite creating several opportunities, the Babasiga Lions were unable to find the equaliser in the first half as thousands of home fans continued to rally behind their side.

Suva’s defence worked tirelessly and looked set to secure all three points as they kept Labasa at bay for most of the match.

However, with only minutes remaining, Labasa midfielder Melvin Mani’s powerful strike took a deflection off Suva defender Remueru Tekiate and wrong-footed the goalkeeper before finding the back of the net to send the home crowd into celebration.

At the final whistle, the score stood at 1-all.

Speaking after the match, Labasa coach Alvin Chand praised his players for their determination and never-say-die attitude.

He says he had faith in his players and knew they could fight back after working hard throughout the match.

Chand says the message at half-time was clear as they needed goals in the second half, and the players responded.

He also thanked the thousands of supporters who stayed until the final whistle to cheer on the team.

In other matches today, Lautoka thrashed Nasinu 4-1, Ba also beat Nadroga 4-1 and Rewa beat Navua 2-1.

In tomorrow’s matches, Nasinu takes on Nadroga at midday, Rewa takes on Suva at 2pm, Ba faces Lautoka at 4pm while hosts Labasa takes on Navua at 6pm.

item.title: Ba open Fiji FACT campaign with 4-1 win over Nadroga
item.description: Ba secured all three points in its opening match of the BiC Fiji FACT tournament after defeating Nadroga 4-1. The Men in Black got off to a strong start in the competition, but it was a tough contest in the first half as both teams were locked at nil-all heading into the break.
item.content:encoded:
Ba secured all three points in its opening match of the BiC Fiji FACT tournament after defeating Nadroga 4-1.

The Men in Black got off to a strong start in the competition, but it was a tough contest in the first half as both teams were locked at nil-all heading into the break.

Nadroga's defence stood firm and frustrated Ba's attacking efforts throughout the opening 45 minutes.

However, Ba came out firing in the second half.

John Alick opened the scoring in the 48th minute before Ryan Naresh doubled the lead in the 55th minute. Antonio Dogalau then took control of the match, scoring two quick goals in the 59th and 61st minutes to put Ba in a commanding position.

Nadroga managed to pull one back through Kehinde Adejumo, but it was not enough to stop Ba from claiming an important victory.

Speaking after the match, Ba President Pranil Dayal says he was proud of the team's performance.

Dayal says the team was focused on winning all its pool matches and maintaining momentum throughout the tournament.

He also call on Ba supporters to travel to Labasa and continue backing the team.

Dayal shares his message to the players was to perform at their best, keep scoring goals and stay focused on the team's objectives.

Ba now takes an early lead in its pool campaign with three valuable points from its opening match.

item.title: Hurricanes, Chiefs chase first Super Rugby title in over a decade
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/06/2026 The Hurricanes and the Chiefs will both be looking to end lengthy title droughts when they meet in the Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific final tomorrow night.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/06/2026

The Hurricanes and the Chiefs will both be looking to end lengthy title droughts when they meet in the Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific final tomorrow night.

The two sides have been among the competition's standout teams this season, showcasing their attacking prowess in dominant semi-final victories.

The Chiefs are no strangers to the big stage, having appeared in the last three finals. However, they were beaten on each occasion and will be determined to finally break through.

Despite their recent finals experience, the Chiefs once again enter the decider as underdogs. They will, however, take confidence from their victory over the Hurricanes earlier this season.

The Chiefs' last Super Rugby title came in 2013 when they defeated the Brumbies 27-22, while the Hurricanes last lifted the trophy in 2016.

The Hurricanes will host the Chiefs at 7.05pm tomorrow.

item.title: Lautoka open Fiji FACT campaign with 4-1 win over Nasinu
item.description: Nilufa Buksh , Mohammed Feroz · 19/06/2026 Khelvin Civil Engineering Lautoka FC made a strong start to their BiC Fiji FACT campaign, defeating Nasinu FC 4-1 in their opening match at Subrail Park in Labasa.
item.content:encoded:
Nilufa Buksh , Mohammed Feroz · 19/06/2026

Khelvin Civil Engineering Lautoka FC made a strong start to their BiC Fiji FACT campaign, defeating Nasinu FC 4-1 in their opening match at Subrail Park in Labasa.

The Blues dominated the first half and took a 3-1 lead into the break through a brace from Valeni Rasorewa in the 9th and 15th minutes, while Rusiate Matarerega added a third goal in the 31st minute.

Lautoka remained in control after the restart, with Matarerega scoring his second goal of the match to extend their lead to 4-1.

The win gives Lautoka an ideal start to the tournament as they look to challenge for this year's BiC Fiji FACT title.

Meanwhile, in other matches Rewa will face Navua at 5pm and Labasa will take on Suva at 7.30pm.

item.title: Mexico secure 1-0 win over South Korea in Group A clash
item.description: Mexico secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory over South Korea in their FIFA World Cup Group A clash. At halftime, both teams were level at nil-all.
item.content:encoded:
Mexico secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory over South Korea in their FIFA World Cup Group A clash.

At halftime, both teams were level at nil-all.

South Korea pressed for an equaliser in the closing stages, but Mexico's defence stood strong to preserve the lead and claim all three points.

The victory boosts Mexico's chances of progressing from Group A, while South Korea will look to bounce back in their next match.

Meanwhile, in other matches South Africa and Czechia played out a 1-1 draw, Switzerland defeated Bosnia and Herzegovina 4-1, Canada thrashed Qatar 6-0, while Mexico edged South Korea 1-0 in their FIFA World Cup group matches.

Tomorrow's FIFA World Cup action will see the USA take on Australia at 7am, Scotland face Morocco at 10am, Brazil meet Haiti at 1pm, and Türkiye play Paraguay at 4pm Fiji time.

item.title: Drua Women target strong start against Brumbies
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/06/2026 The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women are looking to make a strong start when they host the Brumbies in Ba tomorrow in round 3 of the Super Rugby Women's competition.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/06/2026

The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women are looking to make a strong start when they host the Brumbies in Ba tomorrow in round 3 of the Super Rugby Women's competition.

Coach Mike Legge says the team reviewed last weekend's match and identified their slow start as an area that needs improvement.

While he acknowledged the side's resilience in coming from behind to beat the Force, Legge says they cannot afford to rely on another comeback against the Brumbies.

He adds that the team has also been working on tightening its defence around the ruck.

The coach says all the girls are fit and healthy and ready to roll tomorrow.

The Drua Women and the Brumbies are both on four points in the standings, while the Waratahs sit at the top of the table with eight points.

The Drua Women will face the Brumbies at 2.05pm tomorrow at 4R Stadium in Ba.

item.title: Canada secure dominant 6-0 victory over Qatar in Group B clash
item.description: Canada produced a dominant performance to defeat Qatar 6-0 in their FIFA World Cup Group B clash. The Canadians were already in complete control by halftime, taking a comfortable 3-0 lead into the break through goals from Cyle Larin and Jonathan David, who scored twice in the opening half.
item.content:encoded:
Canada produced a dominant performance to defeat Qatar 6-0 in their FIFA World Cup Group B clash.

The Canadians were already in complete control by halftime, taking a comfortable 3-0 lead into the break through goals from Cyle Larin and Jonathan David, who scored twice in the opening half.

Qatar's task became even more difficult after Homam El Amin was sent off in the 33rd minute, leaving his side to play most of the match with 10 men.

Canada continued to pile on the pressure in the second half as Niko Saliba added a fourth goal before an own goal from Mohammad Manai further increased the lead.

Jonathan David completed a memorable night by scoring his third goal in stoppage time to secure a hat-trick and seal Canada's biggest win of the tournament so far.

The emphatic victory boosts Canada's chances of progressing from Group B, while Qatar will now need a strong response in their remaining matches.

item.title: Full house expected as Hurricanes host Chiefs in Super Rugby final tomorrow
item.description: The Hurricanes have received a major boost ahead of tomorrow's Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific Grand Final against the Chiefs, wit

item.title: Hurricanes and Chiefs set for Super Rugby Pacific title showdown
item.description: The stage is set for a thrilling finale to the 2026 Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific season, with the Hurricanes and Chiefs set to
